forecasts.nextMove: Trimmed mean inflation to stay above target until mid-2028

rba.note: Two hikes in 2026. May hike 60% priced. RBA says inflation above target until mid-2028. Rates staying high.

employment.note: March data improved to 4.1% from 4.3% in Feb. Labour market more resilient than expected.

inflation.note: Still well above 2-3% target. Housing +7.2%, electricity distortions. RBA expects above-target until mid-2028.

wages.note: Wages 3.4% vs inflation 3.7% = real wages still slightly negative.

migration.forecast2026: Treasury: NOM to 260,000 in 2025-26, settling near 225,000 by 2026-27
migration.note: 311k NOM still elevated. WA and QLD growing fastest. Brisbane added 58,200 residents (60% from overseas migration).

approvals.note: Persistent undersupply. 18% below 240k/yr target. Good for existing property values.

vacancy.note: National vacancy fell to 1.0% in March — tightest on record. Perth 0.6%, Adelaide 0.5%, Brisbane 0.6%.

medians.note: Combined capital median now $1.025M. Perth +2.5% monthly (strongest). Sydney/Melbourne flat or dipping. March: Perth +2.5%, Brisbane +1.8%, Adelaide +1.2%.

clearanceRates.note: National clearance 56.5% (down from 64.3% prior week). Listings surging as holidays end. Lower clearance = more buyer leverage.

lending.apraChanges: DTI cap: max 20% of new loans at 6x+ income, effective Feb 2026. 3% serviceability buffer retained.
lending.note: Investor rates 5.59-6.60%. Assessment rate ~9% squeezes borrowing. DTI cap limits leverage. Rates may go higher in May.

risks.impact: Each 25bp reduces borrowing capacity ~3%. Third hike in 2026.

risks.risk: Inflation sticky above target until mid-2028
risks.impact: No rate relief for 2+ years. RBA's own forecast.
Could push energy prices higher, forcing more hikes.
195k approvals vs 240k target. Structural shortage supports prices.
56.5% national clearance = more listings than buyers. Could signal softening.

summary: Macro environment tightening: RBA at 4.10% with May hike likely, inflation 3.7% stuck above target, and RBA guidance says rates stay high until mid-2028. However structural tailwinds persist: national vacancy hit record-low 1.0%, approvals 18% below target, and Perth/Brisbane/Adelaide continue strong monthly growth. Falling clearance rates (56.5%) actually help buyers — more negotiation power. For a $800k growth investor, target established suburbs in WA/QLD where vacancy <1% and infrastructure is DELIVERING (not just announced).
warnings: May 5 RBA meeting: likely +25bp to 4.35% — reassess serviceability
Clearance rates falling = market softening in Sydney/Melbourne. Good for buyers but watch for price drops
Perth +2.5% monthly is the hottest capital — act fast on sub-$800k suburbs before they price out
APRA DTI cap 6x income — confirm borrowing capacity with broker before making offers
